blocks.text:
Ok here comes the fun! The adventure starts before the wedding!

As Patmos doesn't have an airport, you'll first need to fly to **Kos (KGS)**, **Rhodes (RHO)** or **Athens (ATH)** and then continue by ferry. Patmos is connected by ferry with all three destinations.
Flying to Kos or Rhodes

For many of our guests, Kos or Rhodes will be the easiest option.

Ryanair and other airlines usually release their summer schedules from January onwards. Depending on where you're travelling from, return flights can often be found from around €100.

Once you arrive, there are buses and taxis connecting the airports to the ferry ports. We will try to post the ferry schedule link here. In Rhodes, Google Maps works perfectly.
Flying to Athens

Athens is often one of the cheapest options.

If you fly into Athens, make sure to allow enough time to get from the airport to the Port of Piraeus:
Ferry to Patmos

After landing, you'll need to catch a ferry to Patmos.

We strongly recommend leaving at least **1–2 hours between your flight arrival and ferry departure**. Island travel is usually relaxed, but ferries won't wait if your flight is delayed!
Approximate Ferry Times

items: **From Rhodes:** 4–7 hours (depending on which boat and which day you choose)

blocks.text: We'll share more detailed ferry information and recommended connections once the summer schedules are released.

blocks.title: 🏛️ A Small Greek Island Tip
blocks.text:
Patmos is not the easiest island to get to—and that's part of what makes it special.

Don't schedule tight connections. Grab a coffee at the port (freddooooo espressooo please!), enjoy the ferry ride, look at the sea, and switch into island mode. The pace is slower here, and that's exactly why we love it.

June is one of our favourite times on Patmos: warm weather, beautiful swimming conditions, and a much more relaxed atmosphere than the peak summer months.
We're currently arranging special rates with selected hotels and apartments in Skala where the party will be, and will update this section as soon as everything is confirmed.
We highly recommend renting:

Patmos is small, but having your own transportation makes it easy to discover beautiful beaches, viewpoints, and hidden corners of the island.

There is a bus as well with some options available!

Taxis are limited and a bit expensive, especially during the summer, so having your own wheels can be very handy.
These are some of our personal favourites:
(Google Maps links coming soon)
Patmos is famous for its peaceful atmosphere, beautiful beaches, and unique history.
Must-See Places

items: Monastery of Saint John the Theologian (UNESCO World Heritage Site)
Wander around the beautiful streets of Chora (Pirate History)

blocks.text: Beaches & Nature

items: Psili Ammos — a day here is always magical! Try to start early: drive 15 min then walk 30 min through the mountain. Easy path with beautiful views.
Ancient Acropolis hike for sunrise or sunset

blocks.text: To make everything comfortable, we'll arrange transportation for parts of the wedding weekend.

**If you would like a seat on the bus on Friday and/or Saturday, please send us a WhatsApp message and let us know.**

More details about pick-up points and timings will follow closer to the wedding.

finalNote.heading: A Final Note ❤️
finalNote.text:
We know that getting to Patmos takes a little more effort than flying directly to a big city or resort.

Thank you for making the journey.

We're incredibly grateful that you'll be travelling all this way to celebrate with us, and we can't wait to share our favourite island, our favourite places, and one unforgettable weekend with all of you. 🏛️✨🌊🥂
